zotero.abstractNote: Implicit neural representations have shown great potential for 3D shape reconstruction. However, existing methods are typically trained on a fixed set of object classes and struggle to generalize to novel categories. In this paper, we propose Any-Shot GIN, a framework that enables implicit networks to reconstruct 3D shapes of novel classes given only a few observations. Our approach leverages a meta-learning strategy to learn a shape prior that can be adapted to unseen categories with minimal supervision. We demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on few-shot 3D reconstruction tasks across various datasets.
